human_verification.notes: Reviewed the chemicals, plastics, and materials manufacturing chemical-quality package for specialty-boundary quality, authority-source quality, calibration and traceability realism, hold and release discipline, field-condition escalation, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to chemical-sector quality evidence review, laboratory and calibration traceability, hold and release-boundary handling, and changed-condition escalation in industrial operations.

Authority sources cover OSHA PSM regulation and enforcement, EPA RMP rule and current fact sheets, NIST measurement and traceability guidance, and ACC Responsible Care workflow discipline with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, repeat defects, control bypass requests, threshold and inspection gaps, field-condition changes, authority-side signoff refusal, low-confidence handling, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, adjacent-lane routing, and unsupported closure attempts.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/chemicals-plastics-and-materials-manufacturing/chemical-quality-specialist/ to preserve the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final QA release, engineering authority, EHS authority, regulatory reporting, and maintenance execution.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated chemicals-sector quality overlay where final QA, engineering, EHS, regulatory-reporting, and legal authority remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
